The given question is followed by three statements labelled I, II and III. You have to study the question and all the three statements given to decide whether any information provide in the statement(s) is/are redundant and can be dispensed with while answering the given question.
What is the average salary of 15 employees?
1. Average salary of 7 clerical cadre (out of the 15 employees) is
2. Average salary of 5 officer cadre (out of the 15 employees) is
3. Average salary of the 3 sub-staff employees (out of the 15 employees) is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q8 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A group of six friends named Peter, Qadir, Roger, Shah, Tarun and Usman have different height
and weight. They are ranked such that the heaviest is ranked 1 and the tallest is ranked 1 in
respective category. Some more details are known:
I. Peter is shorter but heavier than Shah
II. Roger and Tarun are heavier but shorter than Peter and Usman
III. Shah is heavier but shorter than Qadir but neither of them is either the tallest or the heaviest.
The rank of Qadir, if all are arranged in descending order of their height and the tallest is given rank 1, is __________.

NAT
Correct answer: 2
Solution

2

Federer’s fifth grand slam win prompted a reporter to ask whether he was the best ever. Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Option ‘c’ is the most appropriate way of completing the given paragraph.

Q16 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The electron tunneling occurs in a Tunnel diode when which of the following conditions is/are satisfied?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
Electron tunneling occurs in highly doped semiconductor which results in narrow width of energy barrier.
For each tunneling carrier, there must be a filled energy state from which it can be drawn on the
source side of the barrier and an available empty state of the same energy level to which it can go
on the other side of the barrier.

Q17 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

(Assume the diffusion coefficient of electrons is, Dn = 220 cm2/sec).

NAT
Correct answer: 105.6
Solution

105.6 (104 - 107)

Q25 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The type of feedback topology present in the amplifier shown below is

 
 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
We can see that, V0 is voltage across RL and feedback element is R2. Since feedback element is
directly connected to output node V0, it is voltage sampling.
Also the feedback element is connected in shunt with signal source at input. So, shunt mixing.
Thus it represents voltage shunt feedback i.e. shunt - shunt feedback.

NAT
Correct answer: 0
Solution

0 (0 - 0)

Q60 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

An EM wave is incident on a slab of conducting material whose slab width is thinner than its skin depth. Which of the following will not happen?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a, b, c)
Since the slab thickness is less than the skin depth, a significant portion of the EM wave’s energy
is passed on the other side without being heavily attenuated. Hence, options (a), (b) and (c) are not correct.
